_comment: Allium <-> TLA+ binding for the DeltaNet (full vanilla hybrid pipeline)
tla_helpers       — TLA+ symbols that are intentionally NOT invariants:
functions, symmetry sets. Whitelisted so the
divergences       — pairs where a TLA+ INVARIANT must have a different
name from its source Allium invariant. Should stay
empty under normal use. Adding a divergence
bindings_external — Allium invariants that are intentionally NOT
bound by a TLA+ invariant. Either bound by C++
tests, empirical capture, or static analysis.
Recording them here suppresses the forward-drift

bindings_external.bound_by: DFLASH_DIAG=1 stderr trace in test-deltanet-d1-capture; t->type=f32 at np=1 single-token, f16 at np>=2 multi-token
bindings_external.rationale: Storage-precision branch is a property of the build graph emission path, not the kernel state machine; bound by runtime diagnostic

bindings_external.bound_by: data/deltanet/s1-1-rep-identity.json — 128/128 byte-identical at NP=2 across two reps
bindings_external.rationale: Empirical determinism property of the production kernel set; not a state-machine invariant

bindings_external.bound_by: Static analysis of mmq.cuh:4175-4220 mmq_x_best picker
data/deltanet/s2-3-op-level-localization.json — Qcur/Kcur/Vcur/Qcur_normed/Kcur_normed/gate/q IDENTICAL at slot 0 across NP for p3 layer 3, p1 layer 3, p0 layer 19
bindings_external.rationale: Per-prompt empirical capture of CUDA kernel output equivalence; outside the TLA+ state machine which abstracts over layers

bindings_external.bound_by: data/deltanet/s2-3-op-level-localization.json — DeltaNet outputs IDENTICAL at p3 layer 0 across NP
bindings_external.rationale: Same as above; empirical kernel output equivalence

Per-prompt variability is captured at the model-state level (TLA+ models a single prompt's path); separate invocations of the TLA+ model with different FA_ShapeSensitive constants represent different prompts

bindings_external.bound_by: data/phase_dflash_t8/gate7-token-diff-summary.json — NP=1 vs NP=2 byte-identical at token level
bindings_external.rationale: Token-level property emerges from greedy argmax over cumulative drift; the TLA+ model captures per-layer drift but not token-level argmax

bindings_external.bound_by: Negation of WmmaFAShapeSensitiveAtSomeInputs (counterexample to the positive 'batch-invariance' invariant)
bindings_external.rationale: Non-invariants are documented for spec-completeness; not separately bound

bindings_external.bound_by: PerPromptDriftSiteVariability shows first-divergent layer per prompt
PENDING — extension of S2.3 captures to p2, p4, p5 layer 3
bindings_external.rationale: Open invariant; not yet bound. Tracks pending empirical work

bindings_external.bound_by: PENDING — extension of S2.3 captures to p0 layers 3, 7, 11, 15 (per-device il_cb)
PENDING — kernel-internal reading of fattn-wmma-f16.cuh to identify the specific batch-shape-dependent computation
bindings_external.rationale: Open invariant; not yet bound. The TLA+ model abstracts the FA kernel as a non-deterministic action; the internal mechanism becomes a refinement invariant once identified
